methodology.note: ClawHub publicly exposes plugin sorting by downloads, installs, and stars, but detail SSR payloads still expose little or no readable public count data for many plugins. This report therefore weights board position, verification quality, scan status, category fit, and author production density.

Twitter Command Center Search Post Interact

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw memory plugin powered by Gralkor (Graphiti + FalkorDB). Installs a plugin that supervises the Gralkor Python server and wires its HTTP API into OpenClaw hooks and tools.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, runtime instructions, and configuration are consistent with a memory plugin that supervises a local Gralkor Python server and wires its HTTP API into OpenClaw; it does require LLM API keys and a writable data directory and will spawn a local Python process, which are expected for this purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Hivemind — cloud-backed persistent shared memory for AI agents, powered by DeepLake

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and storage/network behavior match its stated purpose: a Deeplake-backed shared memory plugin that stores conversations to api.deeplake.ai and keeps a long‑lived token under ~/.deeplake/credentials.json.

compositeTop.summary: Native OpenClaw channel plugin for agentschatapp

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This package appears to be a legitimate OpenClaw channel plugin for Agents Chat and its code, runtime instructions, and resource needs are consistent with that purpose.

compositeTop.summary: SEO & GEO Skills Library

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This is a content-only SEO/GEO skills library whose requested assets and instructions are coherent with its purpose; it uses optional external connectors and local persistent memory which you should review before enabling in sensitive environments.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw channel plugin: WhatsApp Cloud API via imBee routing (WebSocket + signed webhooks).

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The code and config match the stated purpose (an OpenClaw channel that routes WhatsApp via an imBee routing server); no obvious misdirection or hidden endpoints, but pay attention to where the apiKey is stored and which routing server you point it at.

compositeTop.summary: ZeroAPI - balanced benchmark-aware routing for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code, file access, and runtime behavior are consistent with a routing/benchmarking plugin for OpenClaw; it reads/writes config under ~/.openclaw and can adjust session auth for routing, which is expected for this purpose.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw Opik exporter — sends LLM traces to Opik

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This plugin appears to do what it claims (export OpenClaw traces to Opik); code and docs align with that purpose, but there are privacy-relevant behaviors and small metadata inconsistencies you should be aware of before enabling it in a production gateway.

compositeTop.summary: ClawRecipes plugin for OpenClaw (markdown recipes -> scaffold agents/teams)

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, docs, and runtime instructions align with its stated purpose (scaffolding teams/agents and running file-first workflows), but it legitimately reads OpenClaw config/env and can install/execute other skills — review those behaviors and keys before enabling auto-install or wide deployment.

compositeTop.summary: Mem0 memory backend for OpenClaw — platform or self-hosted open-source

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin appears to be a legitimate Mem0-backed memory backend, but the package metadata and SKILL.md disagree about required credentials and the runtime instructions include prompt-injection behavior and persistent local storage — review before installing.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin — AI video production via a local OpenMontage workspace

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill is internally coherent: it is a skills-only package that teaches an OpenClaw agent how to delegate video jobs to a local OpenMontage workspace and its requirements and instructions match that purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Official Comment.io plugin for OpenClaw — @mention notifications and agent API guidance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and docs match its claimed purpose (Comment.io integration and API guidance) but it injects agent secrets and live API guidance directly into the agent's system prompt/context — a high-risk design choice that is coherent with its goals but potentially exposes sensitive tokens and gives the model undue control over prompt content.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw Context Engine 人格守护插件（内置网关重启测试）

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and declared behavior align with a 'personality guardian' (reading/writing MEMORY.md, snapshots, calling memory_search, and hooking into prompt construction), but it contains prompt-injection patterns and has the ability to inject system-level lock tags and persist data to the agent workspace — a high-impact capability that warrants careful review before enabling.

compositeTop.summary: WTT channel plugin for OpenClaw — real-time Agent communication via Topics

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code, instructions, and requirements are consistent with a messaging channel plugin: it needs agent credentials configured in OpenClaw, writes the plugin account into OpenClaw's config, can restart the gateway, and downloads media referenced in topic backfill — nothing in the bundle contradicts its stated purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-perplexity-sonar-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it wraps a bundled Python client that calls AIsa's API and only asks for the AISA_API_KEY and python3 as declared.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-twitter`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package appears to do what it claims — a Python-based AIsa-backed Twitter/X client requiring an AISA_API_KEY — with no unexpected credential requests or arbitrary downloads, though there are minor metadata inconsistencies to note.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `crypto-market-data`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it implements a crypto market-data client that requires a single AISA_API_KEY and python3, and its files and instructions align with that purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `multi-source-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it implements a search client that requires a single AISA_API_KEY and python3, and its code and instructions align with the stated search/synthesis purpose.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw NIM (NetEase IM) channel plugin

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin is mostly consistent with a NetEase IM channel integration, but it takes broad runtime control (overrides global unhandledRejection handling) and documents optional streaming-to-remote-server behavior and environment variables that are not declared—these are scope and privilege concerns you should review before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `scholar-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package, manifests, SKILL.md, and included Python client are consistent: the skill requires a single AISA_API_KEY and runs a local Python CLI that calls aisa.one APIs to perform searches; nothing in the bundle requests unrelated credentials, installs remote code, or reads unexpected local data.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This package is coherent: it requires an AISA_API_KEY and uses a bundled Python client to call AIsa's search APIs — nothing in the files requests unrelated credentials or system access.

plugins.displayName: AI Image Generator & Editor — Nanobanana, GPT Image, ComfyUI
plugins.summary: MeiGen AI Design — Claude Code Plugin

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's files and instructions broadly match an AI image-generation plugin, but there are inconsistent runtime instructions and a PostToolUse hook that auto-opens saved files which are surprising and worth reviewing before installing.

plugins.summary: PLUR memory plugin for OpenClaw — persistent learning across sessions

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin is generally coherent with a local persistent-memory purpose, but it auto-modifies OpenClaw configuration and the agent's system prompt (telling the agent not to ask permission), and it wires in an npx-run MCP server — behaviors users should review before installing.

plugins.summary: Post tweets, reply, like, retweet, follow, DM & more from OpenClaw - full X/Twitter automation via Xquik. 111 endpoints, reads from $0.00015/call.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's declared functionality (X/Twitter automation via Xquik) is largely coherent with its code, but there are a few implementation and metadata inconsistencies and a sandboxed code-execution surface that merit caution before installing.

plugins.summary: Ops-focused n8n tools (list, inspect, trigger, validate, save) for Claude-compatible clients. MCP server + first-class OpenClaw plugin.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package appears to do what its README and SKILL.md say — it’s an n8n ops toolset (list/inspect/trigger/validate/edit) that talks to your n8n instance via its public API key — but the published metadata and packaging contain small inconsistencies you should be aware of before installing.

plugins.summary: AgentChat for OpenClaw — give your agent its own chat network. DM other agents, save contacts, join group chats, with presence and real-time WebSocket. Not a pipe to humans; a peer-to-peer messaging platform for autonomous agents.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package largely implements a peer-to-peer agent chat channel as claimed, but there are notable inconsistencies (metadata vs runtime), and a persistent system-prompt injection behaviour plus config-writing that you should understand before installing.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin for live hotel and vacation rental search via the StayFinder service. Returns real-time pricing, availability, and Expedia booking redirect links — no scraping, no browser automation.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code and runtime instructions largely match its stated purpose (live lodging search with OTP signup), but the package metadata omits the credential file/config path and a couple of environment assumptions — a mismatch worth reviewing before install.

plugins.summary: Reference pull-only OpenClaw plugin runtime for Bricks platform APIs

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code largely matches its stated purpose (a Bricks pull-only OpenClaw channel) but registry metadata omits required configuration, the runtime persists chat state to disk, and the code decodes JWT claims without verifying signatures — these inconsistencies warrant caution before installing.

plugins.summary: Memory plugin for OpenClaw. Agent's memories strengthen through use, fade when unused, and consolidate overnight.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package mostly implements a coherent associative-memory plugin, but the SKILL.md content is not human-readable (it contains package metadata with possible unicode-control characters), the registry metadata claims 'instruction-only' while compiled code is bundled, and the plugin performs broad filesystem and database IO — these inconsistencies merit caution before installing.

plugins.summary: Venice.ai image and video generation for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and runtime behavior match its stated goal (call Venice.ai to generate images/videos), but there are several documentation/configuration inconsistencies and small mismatches you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: Venice.ai image generation for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin appears to implement Venice.ai image generation as described, but there are inconsistent provider/config names and a mismatch between the documentation and the plugin's config schema that could cause misconfiguration or confusion.

plugins.summary: SteamedClaw plugin: register_agent + queue_match + get_turn + take_turn + get_rules + get_strategy tools backed by /ws/agent and /ws/game push sockets.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, docs, and runtime instructions are consistent with a SteamedClaw WebSocket-based game-playing integration; it asks for no unrelated credentials and its filesystem/network activity matches the stated purpose.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `stock-rumors`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Query stocks, crypto, prediction markets, and portfolio research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs market data, screening, price history, or investment analysis. Supports research and analysis-ready outputs.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package generally matches its stated purpose (stock/rumor scanning using an AIsa API key) but the runtime instructions and script contain mismatches (claims to 'fetch live data', undeclared environment variables, and reliance on an LLM to 'scan' sources) that warrant caution before installing or running with a real API key.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `twitter-command-center-search-post`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is functionally aligned with its Twitter/X posting/search purpose, but it relies on an external relay (api.aisa.one) that will receive your AISA_API_KEY and any uploaded media, and the runtime reads a few environment variables that the manifest/SKILL.md don't fully declare — you should review/trust the relay before installing.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `x-intelligence-automation`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package largely does what it says (Twitter/X research, monitoring, and OAuth-backed posting via an AIsa relay) but there are metadata inconsistencies and the skill will upload user content and attachments to a third‑party relay (api.aisa.one), so you should verify you trust that service before installing.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `youtube`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search YouTube videos, channels, rankings, and trends through AIsa. Use when: the user needs YouTube research, competitor scouting, or content discovery. Supports video discovery and SERP-style analysis.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package mostly does what it says (YouTube research via AIsa) but the manifest/registry metadata and the bundled skill disagree about required environment variables and runtime, and the skill will send your AISA_API_KEY to api.aisa.one — confirm you trust that upstream and the pkg metadata before installing.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `youtube-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally inconsistent: it is named 'Youtube Search' but the bundled skill and manifests describe Twitter/X search/posting and require an AISA_API_KEY; functionality and naming don't line up even though the runtime footprint itself is small.

plugins.summary: quantacomput

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill is extremely minimal and incoherent: it provides almost no instructions, a vague name/description that don't explain the included CSV files, and leaves broad discretion to the agent — not obviously malicious but too ambiguous to trust without clarification.

rankingMechanics: ClawHub plugin discovery now spans separate downloads, installs, and stars boards.
Type filters such as code-plugin vs bundle-plugin change how users narrow the catalog before click-through.
Verified-only and executes-code filters function as practical trust and risk gates, not cosmetic toggles.
Manifest/runtime coherence matters because detail pages surface runtime ID, capabilities, compatibility, and security scan data together.
breakoutMechanics: Task-first or system-first titles outperform abstract product names.
Code plugins lead when they expose a real runtime integration or operational action.
Focused sibling ladders outperform scattered one-off plugin releases.
Security, governance, commerce, messaging, and workflow connectors remain the most repeatable plugin lanes.
trustMechanics: Source-linked provenance reduces cold-start trust friction.
Security Scan, VirusTotal, and OpenClaw verdicts are now part of the conversion surface.
Capability tags, runtimeId, and README copy should describe the same real behavior.
Side effects such as config writes, gateway restarts, remote fetches, or persistent storage need to be declared explicitly.
publishMoves: Package for all three boards instead of optimizing for a single list view.
Choose code-plugin vs bundle-plugin based on the true runtime surface, not on convenience.
Keep one flagship plugin plus narrower siblings in the same lane to compound author trust.
Align title, description, manifest, README, and shipped files before upload.

commonFactors: Plugin names win when they describe a concrete external system or admin action.
Source-linked verification and scan coherence matter more than decorative copy.
Code plugins dominate because users expect real runtime leverage from plugins.
High-potential plugins explain side effects, setup paths, and trust boundaries clearly.
Winning authors build focused plugin factories around one lane instead of shipping disconnected experiments.
authorPatterns: High-output authors cluster around a single theme and release adjacent plugins under one identity.
Authors that publish governance, communication, or growth plugins create a stronger operational brand than generic “AI helper” authors.
A small number of authors already show factory behavior by shipping multiple code plugins with similar install semantics.
aisaMoves: Turn business-growth, CRM, logistics, storage, and governance plugins into remote command-center APIs first.
Use one flagship API lane plus 2-4 narrower SKU variants to create a plugin-to-API factory.
Keep memory-core and low-level routing plugins as later-stage infrastructure rather than first-wave AISA products.
